rules:
Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 cards, you can't play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragonblade
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.
This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

attacks.name: Attack Command
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

rules:
Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent reveals his or her hand and shuffles all Item cards found there into his or her deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Item cards your opponent shuffled into his or her deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Windfall
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Jet Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Flight
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Barrier Break
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Helix Force
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raiden Knuckle
attacks.effect: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Team Plasma Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunderous Noise
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Boiler
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamite Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.
This card provides Colorless Energy.

Search your deck for a Team Plasma Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon this card is attached to is a Team Plasma Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 cards, you can't play this card.) Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't choose a card you discarded with the effect of this card.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put a Team Plasma card from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent reveals his or her hand and shuffles all Item cards found there into his or her deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Item cards your opponent shuffled into his or her deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Whenever any player attaches an Energy from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon (excluding Team Plasma Pokémon) put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Windfall
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Jet Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strong Breeze
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

flavorText: It never stops attacking even if it is injured. It fusses over the shape of its comb.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.
Because they are weak individually, they form groups. However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

attacks.name: Imittack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. If this Pokémon has the necessary Energy to use that attack, use it as this attack.

flavorText: It can freely change its body's color. The zigzag pattern on its belly doesn't change, however.

attacks.name: Max Milk
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that kids who drink Miltank's milk grow up to become hearty, healthy adults.

attacks.name: Powerful Vision
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

attacks.name: Dual Draw
attacks.effect: Each player draws 2 cards.

flavorText: It marks time precisely. Some countries consider it to be a wise friend, versed in the world's ways.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for 3 Pokémon of different types that evolve from Eevee. Reveal them and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Transfer Junk
attacks.effect: Put a Team Plasma Pokémon, a Team Plasma Trainer card, and a Team Plasma Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: With its long fangs, this surprisingly violent Pokémon can gnaw away even thick concrete with ease.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It gnaws on hard objects to wear down its fangs, which grow constantly during its lifetime.

attacks.name: Mach Flight
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Barrier Break
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Vortex
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of Water Energy cards and Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile. Then, shuffle all of those cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Tri Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives in caves on the seafloor and creates giant whirlpools every time it moves.

attacks.name: Deafen
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Healwing
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If its body takes on an aura, the weather changes instantly. It is said to live in seas and lakes.

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is called the "Mirage Pokémon" because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Big Ol' Bite
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

attacks.name: Metal Defender
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: The iron it ingested with the soil it swallowed transformed its body and made it harder than diamonds.

attacks.name: Tractorbeam
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Obsidian Fang
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path!

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Lacking sight, it's unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Lacking sight, it's unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

attacks.name: Dragon Slayer
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Dragon Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.
Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.
Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

attacks.name: Piston Headbutt
attacks.effect: Move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Very violent Pokémon, they try to clamp down on anything that moves in front of their eyes.
Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

attacks.name: Gentle Slap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: They live hidden under hot desert sands in order to keep their body temperature from dropping.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.effect: Does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fearsome Shadow
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: It appears from deep in the mountains to warn people about upcoming disasters it has sensed with its horn.

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Vilify
attacks.effect: Discard as many Pokémon as you like from your hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Pokémon you discarded.

flavorText: They communicate by clawing signs in boulders and work together to surround enemies.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A smart and sneaky Pokémon, it makes its opponents flinch by suddenly showing the claws hidden in its paws.
When exposed to the moon's aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it's filled with a mysterious power.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Pivot Throw
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It loves to match power with big-bodied Pokémon. It can knock a truck flying with its arm thrusts.

attacks.name: Continuous Slap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Opening its large mouth, it ingests massive amounts of soil and creates long tunnels.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.effect: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.
It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

attacks.name: Lovestrike
attacks.effect: Does 40 more damage for each Nidoqueen on your Bench.

flavorText: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

attacks.name: Elongating Arms
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Crazy Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

attacks.name: Slap of Misfortune
attacks.effect: Whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her next turn, treat it as tails.

flavorText: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.
These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life.

attacks.name: Transfer Pain
attacks.effect: Move 1 damage counter from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life.

attacks.name: Helix Force
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: With four linked brains, it's more intelligent than a supercomputer, and it uses calculations to analyze foes.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its two brains are linked, amplifying its psychic power.

attacks.name: Calculate
attacks.effect: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: Its cells are all magnetic, and it communicates with others by using magnetic pulses.

attacks.name: Shadow Cage
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

attacks.name: Psy Alert
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Shadow Ball
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its fur is so sensitive, it can feel minute shifts in the air and predict the weather…and its foes' thoughts.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

attacks.name: Poison Suction
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sludge Crash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It's so stinky! Muk's body contains toxic elements, and any plant will wilt when it passes by.

attacks.name: Lure Poison
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has a violent disposition and stabs foes with its horn, which oozes poison upon impact.

attacks.name: Hit Back
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It scans its surroundings by raising its ears out of the grass. Its toxic horn is for protection.

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its entire body is armored with hard scales. It will protect the young in its burrow with its life.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than Nidorino's.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: While it does not prefer to fight, even one drop of the poison it secretes from its barbs can be fatal.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Fusion Bolt
attacks.effect: If Reshiram is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with lightning. It assists those who want to build an ideal world.

attacks.name: Raiden Knuckle
attacks.effect: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Team Plasma Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunderous Noise
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.effect: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Electric Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Special Tackle
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Extreme Current
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Lanturn's light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed "The Deep-Sea Star."
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Electri-Defuse
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, that Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: By storing electricity in its body, it can shoot its bristlelike fur like a barrage of missiles.
It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.
It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

attacks.name: Frost Spear
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Water Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Cryofreeze
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148° F.

attacks.name: ChillMAX
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Cold Breath
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

attacks.name: Surefire Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F.

attacks.name: Seismic Punch
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Splashing Turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: By putting power into its bumps, it creates vibrations and increases the power of its punches.

attacks.name: Suspicious Soundwave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to immobilize its opponents.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans and warns others of danger.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

attacks.name: Mud Gun
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn't care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When walking on land, it covers its body with a poisonous film that keeps its skin from dehydrating.

attacks.name: Refreshing Rain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can't be seen when it melts away into water.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional-medicine ingredients.

attacks.name: Fin Smack
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

attacks.name: Purifying Flame
attacks.effect: Remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fusion Flare
attacks.effect: If Zekrom is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Its flame is usually out, but it starts shining when it absorbs life force from people or Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heat Boiler
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamite Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vengeance
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees F.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: With cells similar to those of plants, it performs photosynthesis inside its body and creates pure air.

attacks.name: Rapid-Fire Needles
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.effect: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 60 more damage and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day's desert heat.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: By storing water in its body, this desert dweller can survive for 30 days without water.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Energy Bloom
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

flavorText: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.
It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

attacks.name: Blockade
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is called "The Walking Jungle." If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.
Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

attacks.name: Swift Sting
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, this attack does 40 more damage, and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

attacks.name: Triple Stab
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.
